name: Volgograd (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the courage and sacrifice of the defenders of Stalingrad in 1942–1943

description: A red field charged centrally with the coat of arms of Volgograd, recalling the city's status as a Hero City of the Great Patriotic War.
history: Volgograd was known as Tsaritsyn until 1925 and as Stalingrad from 1925 to 1961, the latter name forever associated with the decisive Battle of Stalingrad of 1942–1943. The modern city flag was adopted on 6 March 2008 by the Volgograd City Duma; its red field and gold elements recall the city's Hero City status.

features.symbolism: the city's identity as a Hero City of the Soviet Union
